metaTitle: New Brunswick Pest Control | Same-Day NYC Exterminators
metaDescription: Expert pest control in New Brunswick, NJ. Serving Rutgers campus, George Street, & downtown apartments. Same-day service. Call now for fast relief!
introParagraph: The Best Pest Control NYC delivers expert extermination services throughout New Brunswick, from the Rutgers University campus along George Street to the historic downtown apartments near Albany Street and the residential neighborhoods surrounding Boyd Park. Our licensed technicians understand the unique pest pressures facing this college town, where dense student housing, aging infrastructure, and proximity to the Raritan River create ideal conditions for infestations. We offer same-day emergency service to keep your New Brunswick property pest-free year-round.
whyThisAreaSection: New Brunswick's pest challenges stem from its dense urban core mixed with university housing. The aging brick buildings along George Street and Easton Avenue feature outdated plumbing and wall voids that harbor cockroaches and mice. High student turnover in apartments near Rutgers means frequent vacancies and clutter accumulation. The Raritan River corridor and parks like Buccleuch Park attract rodents and mosquitoes. Heavy pedestrian traffic on the downtown shopping district along Albany Street allows pests to hitchhike between businesses and residences, while proximity to Route 1 and the Northeast Corridor rail line introduces pests from neighboring communities.
propertyTypesSection: New Brunswick's housing mix includes pre-war apartment buildings with shared utilities creating pest highways, student-occupied multi-family units with high turnover and maintenance gaps, and Victorian-era rowhomes with basement moisture issues. The George Street corridor features commercial storefronts with residential units above, where restaurant waste attracts rodents that migrate upstairs. Campus-area buildings often have outdated trash management and structural gaps from decades of renovations. Off-campus student housing near College Avenue experiences bed bug introductions from frequent move-ins, while crawlspace foundations in older neighborhoods provide rodent harborage during winter months.
commonPestsSection: German cockroaches dominate New Brunswick apartments, thriving in shared kitchens and bathrooms of student housing. Bed bugs spread rapidly through college-area buildings due to constant resident turnover. Mice infiltrate older buildings each fall, entering through foundation cracks and utility penetrations. Ants invade ground-floor units during spring rains. Residents near the Raritan River report increased mosquito activity in summer, while downtown businesses battle drain flies and occasional rat sightings near dumpsters on Somerset Street and Commercial Avenue.

faqs.question: How much does pest control cost in New Brunswick?
faqs.answer: General pest control in New Brunswick runs $175–$300 per treatment, while cockroach extermination costs $350–$550. Bed bug heat treatments range from $1,200–$3,500 depending on apartment size. Mouse or rat control typically costs $350–$600, with rodent exclusion services ranging $500–$3,000 for older buildings. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing for all New Brunswick properties.

faqs.question: Do you offer same-day pest control in New Brunswick?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide same-day emergency pest control throughout New Brunswick, including the Rutgers campus area, downtown, and surrounding neighborhoods. Our technicians serve properties along George Street, Somerset Street, and Easton Avenue with rapid response times. Emergency services range from $350–$1,000 depending on the pest type and severity. Call before noon for same-day service availability in most New Brunswick locations.

faqs.question: Are your treatments safe for children and pets in apartments?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products with minimal toxicity, ideal for New Brunswick's dense apartment buildings and student housing. Our technicians apply targeted treatments in cracks, crevices, and wall voids rather than broad spraying. Most treatments allow re-entry within 2–4 hours. We offer pet-friendly and child-safe options for families in College Avenue apartments and homes near Boyd Park, with detailed safety instructions provided.

faqs.question: Why are bed bugs so common in New Brunswick apartments?
faqs.answer: New Brunswick's large student population creates constant turnover in rental units, with bed bugs hitchhiking on furniture, luggage, and clothing during move-ins. High-density housing along George Street and College Avenue allows bed bugs to spread between adjacent units through wall voids and electrical conduits. Our bed bug treatments ($1,200–$3,500) include multi-unit inspections to prevent re-infestation in neighboring apartments.

faqs.question: How do mice spread in older New Brunswick buildings?
faqs.answer: Mice exploit foundation cracks, gaps around utility lines, and deteriorated mortar in New Brunswick's pre-war buildings. Shared basements and crawlspaces in Easton Avenue rowhomes provide rodent highways between units. Mice also enter through damaged basement windows and dryer vents. Our mouse control ($350–$600) includes entry point identification, and we offer comprehensive exclusion services ($500–$3,000) to seal vulnerable older construction permanently.

faqs.question: Do you service off-campus Rutgers student housing?
faqs.answer: Yes, we specialize in off-campus student housing throughout New Brunswick, including properties on College Avenue, Hamilton Street, and Easton Avenue. We understand the unique pest challenges of student rentals and work directly with landlords and property managers. Our technicians are experienced with multi-unit treatments and coordinate services to minimize disruption during academic terms. We offer flexible scheduling around class schedules and semester breaks.
